4	  depends_on:

9	# ADR-007: Federation Topology (Star, Active-Spoke Hybrid)
10	
11	**Status:** Accepted
12	**Date:** 2026-05-02
13	**Context:** Each `ddx server` has stable node identity, persisted state, and a
14	project registry (FEAT-020). The web UI (FEAT-021) uses `/nodes/:nodeId/...` as
15	its canonical URL grammar and was anticipated to fan out across nodes in a
16	future "coordinator" model. Story 14 (FEAT-026) makes that future concrete:
17	multiple ddx-server nodes need to present a unified view of beads, runs, and
18	projects across machines, while the spokes remain fully usable on their own.
19	
20	## Decision
21	
22	Federation is a **star topology** with an **active-spoke hybrid** authority
23	model:
24	
25	- One node runs as the **hub** (`ddx server --hub-mode`) and exposes
26	  `/api/federation/*` plus federated read aggregations.
27	- Other nodes run as **spokes** (`ddx server --hub=<host>`) and push
28	  registration + heartbeat to the hub. A node may run as both (`hub_spoke`).
29	- Spokes remain full ddx-servers; if the hub dies, spokes keep working
30	  autonomously and their UIs remain directly reachable as a fallback path.
31	- Reads are aggregated by the hub via fan-out over the spokes' existing
32	  `/graphql` endpoints. Writes are **never broadcast** — the hub may forward a
33	  mutation to the owning spoke, but federation itself is read-only in v1.
34	
35	### Naming Convention
36	
37	Federation always uses **federation / hub / spoke**. Other terms are reserved or
38	avoided:
39	
40	| Term | Status | Reason |
41	|------|--------|--------|
42	| federation, hub, spoke, hub_spoke | Use | Adopted vocabulary for this feature |
43	| coordinator | Avoid | Already used for the per-project bead-landing serializer (FEAT-010 `coordinatorRegistry`, `land_coordinator.go`, `coordinatorMetricsByProject`); overloading the term causes confusion |
44	| primary, replica | Avoid | Implies replication semantics; federation is registration + read fan-out, not data replication |
45	| leader, follower | Avoid | Implies consensus / failover semantics not present in v1 |
46	| master, worker | Avoid | Overloaded with FEAT-006 worker pool |
47	
48	This convention applies to code (package names, identifiers, log messages),
49	APIs (endpoint paths, GraphQL field names, JSON schema), CLI flags, and
50	documentation.
51	
52	## Topology
53	
54	### Star (one hub, N spokes)
55	
56	- v1 fan-out is **O(spokes)** per federated query. Concurrency is bounded
57	  (default 8 in flight) and each spoke call has a per-request timeout (default
58	  2s for register/heartbeat, 5s for read fan-out).
59	- The hub does not relay between spokes; spokes never talk to each other.
60	- Failover is **out of scope**. There is exactly one hub. A new hub is brought
61	  up by an operator restart; spokes will re-register on their next heartbeat
62	  cycle.
63	- Spoke UIs are first-class fallbacks. `/api/node` and CLI status output expose
64	  the spoke's local URL so operators have a direct path when the hub is
65	  unreachable.
66	
67	### Authority: Hybrid (push registration/heartbeat, pull data on demand)
68	
69	**Push side (spoke → hub):**
70	- `POST /api/federation/register` on startup. Registration is **idempotent on
71	  stable `node_id`**: re-register replaces the registry entry by id; URL change
72	  updates the recorded URL; a duplicate `node_id` from a different identity
73	  (mismatched name + new URL) is rejected with a logged conflict and an
74	  operator-actionable error.
75	- `POST /api/federation/heartbeat` every **30s with random jitter** in
76	  `[0, 5s]` to avoid synchronized beats from waking the hub at the same instant.
77	- `POST /api/federation/deregister` on graceful shutdown.
78	
79	**Pull side (hub → spoke):**
80	- The hub fans out to spokes' existing `/graphql` over ts-net for federated
81	  read queries. No new spoke endpoint is added for fan-out beyond what already
82	  exists for direct UI use.
83	
84	**Liveness states:**
85	- `live` — heartbeat within 30s.
86	- `stale` — no heartbeat for ≥ 2 minutes; UI shows a stale badge but the spoke
87	  is still queried in fan-out.
88	- `offline` — fan-out request to the spoke failed (connection refused, ts-net
89	  unreachable, timeout). Distinct from `stale`: a `live` heartbeat does not
90	  guarantee the spoke is reachable from the hub at query time.
91	
92	The hub registry persists at `~/.local/share/ddx/federation-state.json` so the
93	hub survives restarts and rebuilds its view from disk on startup; spokes
94	re-confirm on their next heartbeat.
95	
96	### Discovery: Explicit `--hub=<host>` Over ts-net DNS
97	
98	- `ddx server --hub-mode` exposes `/api/federation/*` on the existing listener.
99	- `ddx server --hub=<host>` registers + heartbeats on startup against
100	  `<host>:<port>`. `<host>` is typically a ts-net hostname (e.g. `eitri`).
101	- Both flags may be combined (`--hub-mode --hub=<other>`) for `hub_spoke`.
102	- mDNS / Tailscale-tag auto-discovery is **rejected for v1**: it couples DDx to
103	  Tailscale ACL policy and adds a discovery surface we don't need yet.
104	- `.ddx.yml` static config is a **follow-up**. When introduced, the
105	  precedence rule is: **flag wins over config** (consistent with ADR-006's
106	  auth-key precedence rule).
107	
108	### ts-net Policy: Default Strict, Explicit Dangerous Opt-out
109	
110	- Default behavior: federation refuses non-loopback, non-ts-net peers. The
111	  policy is enforced at `/api/federation/*` regardless of the listener that
112	  accepted the request. Loopback is permitted to support same-host
113	  `hub_spoke` topologies and integration tests.
114	- `--federation-allow-plain-http` is the explicit opt-out. It is **only valid
115	  when `--hub-mode` is also set**. When enabled:
116	  - The hub accepts plain-HTTP federation traffic (loopback or otherwise).
117	  - The hub emits a warning log on each accepted plain-HTTP registration and
118	    on startup when the flag is in effect, naming the offending peer.
119	  - Spokes that wish to send plain-HTTP must opt in symmetrically (see
120	    ambiguity #1 below — current decision: **both ends must opt in**, so
121	    spokes carry the same flag).
122	- Rationale: making the dangerous mode noisy and explicit is safer than
123	  letting users build worse external tunnels around an over-strict default.
124	  This mirrors ADR-006's posture on transport-layer auth.
125	
126	### Write Routing Contract (Story 15 Hand-off)
127	
128	Federation is **read-only aggregation in v1**. Federated read models expose
129	per-row routing metadata so a future write path can resolve the owning node:
130	
131	| Field | Type | Purpose |
132	|-------|------|---------|
133	| `node_id` | string | Stable id of the spoke that owns this row |
134	| `project_id` | string | Project id within that node |
135	| `project_url` | string | Spoke base URL (for direct fallback) |
136	| `project_path` | string | On-disk path on the owning node |
137	| `write_capability` | enum | `local`, `forwardable`, `read_only` |
138	| `status` | enum | `live`, `stale`, `offline` of the owning node |
139	
140	When Story 15 (server-side prompt execution) lands, the hub may **forward** a
141	mutation to the owning spoke based on this metadata, but it must **never
142	broadcast** writes. ADR-007 documents the contract; Story 15's design
143	references it.
144	
145	## Version Handshake
146	
147	Spokes send a version block on `POST /api/federation/register`:
148	
149	```json
150	{
151	  "node_id": "node-7029e8d6",
152	  "node_name": "bragi",
153	  "url": "https://bragi:7743",
154	  "ddx_version": "0.42.0",
155	  "schema_version": "1",
156	  "graphql_schema_version": "2026-04-29",
157	  "capabilities": ["beads", "runs", "projects", "doc-graph"]
158	}
159	```
160	
161	The hub applies this **compatibility matrix** and either accepts, marks
162	`degraded`, or rejects:
163	
164	| Spoke vs Hub | `schema_version` | `graphql_schema_version` | `ddx_version` (semver) | Result |
165	|---|---|---|---|---|
166	| Same | equal | equal | equal | **accept** (`live`) |
167	| Spoke older patch | equal | equal | older patch only | **accept** (`live`) |
168	| Spoke newer patch | equal | equal | newer patch only | **accept** (`live`) |
169	| Spoke newer minor | equal | newer date | newer minor | **accept as `degraded`** — hub queries only the capability set both sides advertise |
170	| Spoke older minor | equal | older date | older minor | **accept as `degraded`** — hub avoids fields the spoke does not advertise |
171	| Mismatched major | equal | n/a | major mismatch | **reject** at register time, with explicit error |
172	| Mismatched `schema_version` | not equal | n/a | n/a | **reject** at register time |
173	| Missing `capabilities` | n/a | n/a | n/a | **reject** — hub cannot make safe field-level decisions |
174	
175	`degraded` is surfaced in the UI as a yellow badge on the affected spoke and
176	on every row sourced from it. Reject decisions are made at register time so
177	operators see the failure immediately rather than at query time.
178	
179	## Spec Surface (Compatibility Hooks)
180	
181	ADR-007 is implemented across three feature specs:
182	
183	- **FEAT-026** (new) — owns federation end-to-end: registry schema,
184	  hub/spoke endpoints, fan-out client, federated GraphQL fields, UI routes.
185	- **FEAT-020 amendment** — adds `federation-state.json` schema, the
186	  `--hub-mode`, `--hub=<host>`, `--federation-allow-plain-http` flags, and
187	  the `node.federation_role` field on `/api/node`. Compatibility hooks only,
188	  no implementation.
189	- **FEAT-021 amendment** — adds the `/federation` overview route, hub-resolved
190	  `/nodes/:nodeId/...` routing across registered spokes, and `?scope=federation`
191	  on combined views. Compatibility hooks only, no implementation.
192	
193	## Consequences
194	
195	- **Operator clarity:** one hub, N spokes; explicit flags; explicit failure
196	  modes (`stale`, `offline`, `degraded`, conflict-rejected).
197	- **No replication, no consensus:** the hub holds an in-memory + persisted
198	  view of where data lives, not a copy of the data. Loss of the hub state file
199	  is recoverable on the next heartbeat cycle.
200	- **Spoke autonomy:** spokes do not depend on the hub for correctness. Each
201	  spoke UI remains usable; CLI and direct browser access work without the hub.
202	- **Hub fan-out cost:** O(spokes) per federated read. Bounded concurrency and
203	  per-call timeouts cap the cost; a single slow spoke cannot block the view.
204	- **Plain-HTTP escape valve is documented and noisy:** the dangerous mode is
205	  explicit, log-loud, and gated behind both `--hub-mode` and a separate flag.
206	- **Future write path is preserved:** routing metadata fields are part of
207	  every federated row, so Story 15 can layer write forwarding without a v2
208	  schema migration.
209	- **Naming discipline:** `coordinator`, `primary`, `replica`, `leader` are
210	  reserved/avoided so the federation vocabulary does not collide with
211	  pre-existing per-project subsystems.
212	
213	## Alternatives Considered
214	
215	| Alternative | Verdict | Reason |
216	|---|---|---|
217	| Star topology, push-only (spokes push all data) | Rejected | Doubles storage and forces continuous replication; federation use cases are read-aggregation, not redundancy |
218	| Star topology, pull-only (hub polls spokes) | Rejected | Hub does not know about spokes until they push; auto-discovery of spokes is explicitly out of scope |
219	| Mesh topology (every node peers with every other node) | Rejected | O(N²) connections, no clear authority, harder to reason about for v1 |
220	| Single shared SQLite over a network drive | Rejected | Lock contention, no isolation, fragile across machines |
221	| Failover / hot standby hub | Deferred | Operator restart is acceptable for v1; complexity not justified yet |
222	| mDNS / Tailscale-tag spoke discovery | Deferred | Couples DDx to Tailscale ACL policy; explicit `--hub=` is sufficient |
223	
224	## Flagged Ambiguities (resolved here unless noted)
225	
226	1. **Plain-HTTP opt-out scope.** Decision: **both ends must opt in**. A spoke
227	   that wishes to send plain-HTTP carries `--federation-allow-plain-http`; the
228	   hub must also carry it to accept. This keeps the dangerous surface
229	   symmetric and audit-loggable.
230	2. **Version-skew granularity.** Decision: matrix above. Reject on major or
231	   `schema_version` mismatch; mark `degraded` on minor/graphql-schema drift.
232	3. **`scope: LOCAL | FEDERATION` GraphQL migration.** **Open.** v1 ships
233	   parallel `federated*` fields per FEAT-026 design. A future migration to
234	   unify under a `scope` argument is allowed but not committed to a release
235	   in ADR-007.
236	4. **Heartbeat cadence config surface.** Deferred to v1.1: flag, env var, or
237	   `.ddx.yml`. v1 is hard-coded at 30s + jitter.
238	5. **Duplicate `node_id` recovery UX.** v1 hard-rejects with a logged
239	   conflict and an operator-actionable error. Soft-rebind on operator action
240	   is a follow-up.
241

581:## Federation Compatibility Hooks (FEAT-026)
583:When the node runs as a **federation hub** (FEAT-026, ADR-007), the UI gains:
585:- `/federation` — federation overview route showing the hub, its registered

587:  compatibility status (`live` / `degraded` / `rejected`). Owned by FEAT-026.
588:- `/nodes/:nodeId/...` resolves across the federation: when `:nodeId` is a
589:  registered spoke, the hub serves the route from the spoke's federated read
590:  surface. Spoke UIs remain directly reachable as a fallback.
592:  `?scope=federation` query parameter; when set on a hub, the view fans out
593:  across all spokes and tags each row with `node_id`, `project_id`, and a
595:- Rows sourced from a `stale`, `offline`, or `degraded` spoke carry the
596:  matching badge; the page does not fail when a spoke is unreachable.
598:The naming convention from ADR-007 applies to UI copy: prefer
599:**federation / hub / spoke** over `coordinator`, `primary`, `replica`.

commit eea44b4b7fd35f625e52be735d78f2d037248b8a
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 10:03:36 2026 -0400

    docs(helix): break ADR-007 ↔ FEAT-026 cycle [ddx-c739bd73]

    consume them. Removed FEAT-026 from ADR-007.depends_on; ddx doc audit no
    longer reports the federation cycle.

    docs(helix): ADR-007 federation topology + FEAT-026 frame + FEAT-020/021 amendments [ddx-1d4bfbf3]
    
    Adds ADR-007 (Federation Topology: Star, Active-Spoke Hybrid) capturing topology,
    authority model, ts-net policy, plain-HTTP opt-out, write-routing contract,
    naming convention (federation/hub/spoke), and version-handshake compatibility
    matrix. Adds FEAT-026 frame owning federation end-to-end. Amends FEAT-020 with
    federation_role field, federation flags, and federation-state.json reference
    (compatibility hooks only). Amends FEAT-021 with /federation overview,
    hub-resolved /nodes/:nodeId/..., and ?scope=federation on combined views
    (compatibility hooks only).
